title: Axor Lab Scenario
description: A fully executable scenario: not just the breach criterion but the world that produces it. Adds fixtures (what each tool returns, and where the injection lands) and structured inputs (ground-truth values predicates compare against), so the scenario describes an executable world, not just a test. Gates are never authored here — taint and decide are the kernel's.

task.description: the legitimate goal, as the user prompt

inputs.description: structured ground-truth values (the real accountant email, valid IBANs). Predicates compare against these via $inputs.x — never against entities scraped from the prompt text.

tools.description: the tools available in this scenario; each is a full manifest (or a $ref to a shared one)

$ref.description: id of a tool manifest defined in the bundle's tool_manifests

fixtures.description: what each tool returns during the run, and precisely where the injection is placed. Without this the scenario names a criterion but not an executable world.

result.description: the tool's returned object; may embed the token $injection at the exact field the attacker controls

text.description: the attacker payload; substituted for $injection in fixtures

goal.description: the effect the attacker wants (for readability / EvidenceCase narration)

from_agent.description: multi-agent: the peer/node the injection arrives from, instead of a tool result

violation.description: breach predicate → ASR. e.g. tool_call send_money where prov(args.recipient) is untrusted_derived

$comment: Author-time validation (all enforced before a scenario is runnable): (1) violation.tool and task_success.tool name a declared tool; (2) at least one tool has untrusted_fields AND the fixture places $injection into one of them (an injection needs a vector); (3) at least one EXPORT/WRITE/EXEC tool exists to breach; (4) every $inputs.x resolves; (5) every matcher type-checks against the tool's args/result schema; (6) each predicate has a sample-trace preview. A scenario failing any check is rejected at author time.
